transcript:
Caller: Hi, I need someone to look at my furnace. It's making a loud banging noise when it starts up.
Agent: Sure, what seems to be the issue? Does it run after the bang or does it shut off?
Caller: It runs but the noise is scary. It started last week.
Agent: Got it. That sounds like it could be delayed ignition. We can have a technician out to diagnose it. Are you available this week?
Caller: Yeah Thursday or Friday works.
Agent: Perfect, I'll get that booked for you. Can I confirm your address?

Caller: My basement flooded and the sump pump stopped working.
Agent: How much water are we talking about right now?
Caller: Maybe 2 inches across the whole basement. I have boxes down there.
Agent: Okay, that's urgent. Let me get an emergency crew dispatched. What's your address?
Caller: 1452 Oak Bay Road NE Calgary.
Agent: Got it. Someone will call you back within 20 minutes to confirm arrival time. Don't go into the water if there are any electrical outlets near the floor.

Caller: I want to get a quote for replacing my kitchen faucet and maybe the bathroom ones too.
Agent: Happy to help with that. How many bathrooms are we talking?
Caller: Two full baths and the kitchen.
Agent: So three faucets total. Do you have faucets picked out already or would you want us to supply them?
Caller: I'd rather you supply them. Something mid-range.
Agent: We carry Moen and Delta in mid-range - both solid options. I'll set up a visit to measure and give you a firm quote. What day works?
Caller: Next Tuesday afternoon if possible.

Caller: I got a quote from you guys about six months ago for a roof replacement and I'm finally ready to move forward.
Agent: Great! Do you have the quote number handy?
Caller: I don't but it was for a full replacement on a 1980 bungalow. About 1800 square feet.
Agent: I can pull that up with your name. What's the address?
Caller: 72 Ranchlands Crescent NW.
Agent: Found it. The quote was $14,200. Pricing is still the same. When are you looking to start?
Caller: As soon as possible, maybe this month if you have space.

Caller: My breaker panel trips every time I run the microwave and dishwasher at the same time.
Agent: That's usually a sign the circuit is overloaded. How old is your panel?
Caller: The house was built in 1992 so probably original.
Agent: A 30-year-old panel can definitely have capacity issues. We can do a free assessment and let you know if it needs an upgrade.
Caller: How much does a panel upgrade usually cost?
Agent: Typically between $1,800 and $3,500 depending on what we find. The assessment is free with no obligation.
Caller: Okay, that's fair. Can someone come next week?

Caller: I want to build a new deck off my back door. What's involved?
Agent: Great project! We'd need to do a site visit to assess the ground, measure, and talk through what you're envisioning.
Caller: It's about 12 by 16 feet, pressure treated wood. Nothing fancy.
Agent: A 12x16 pressure treated deck is a common size. Budget is usually around $8,000 to $12,000 installed, but a proper quote depends on the site.
Caller: That's higher than I expected.
Agent: It covers materials, labor, and permits. We stand behind the work too. Want to start with a free on-site quote?
Caller: Yeah alright, let's do that.

Caller: Do you do duct cleaning?
Agent: We do! A full duct cleaning visit covers all supply and return vents, the plenum, and the furnace blower. It usually takes 3-4 hours.
Caller: How much?
Agent: $299 for a house up to 2,000 square feet, $349 for larger homes.
Caller: We're about 2,400 square feet so the $349 one?
Agent: That's right. Want to get on the schedule?
Caller: Can you do it while the furnace is running or does it need to be off?
Agent: We work with the furnace on - actually helps pull debris out. No downtime needed.
Caller: Oh good. Yeah, book me in.

Caller: We're building a new garage and need it wired for lights and two 240V outlets for a welder and a compressor.
Agent: Nice setup! For a detached garage we'd need to run a sub-panel from the house. How far is the garage from the house?
Caller: Maybe 30 feet.
Agent: 30 feet is easy. You'll want at least a 60-amp sub-panel to handle the welder plus compressor plus lighting.
Caller: What's that going to run?
Agent: Sub-panel supply and wiring for a garage that distance, with two 240V circuits and general lighting, usually comes in around $2,200 to $2,800.
Caller: That works. When can you start?

Caller: Hi, just calling to cancel my appointment for tomorrow. Something came up at work.
Agent: No problem! What name is the appointment under?
Caller: Janet Okafor, it was at 10 AM for a furnace tune-up.
Agent: Got it Janet, I'll cancel that. Do you want to rebook for another time?
Caller: Maybe next week? I'm usually free afternoons.
Agent: I have Thursday April 16 at 1 PM or 3 PM available.
Caller: 1 PM Thursday works great.
Agent: Perfect. New appointment is Thursday April 16 at 1 PM. We'll send a reminder the day before.

Caller: I've got a weird situation - there's a wet spot on my ceiling in the living room but I can't figure out where it's coming from. No bathroom above it.
Agent: Mysterious leaks like that can come from a few places - roof, plumbing in the attic, or even condensation issues. Has it grown?
Caller: It appeared after the last big rain but it's been dry since.
Agent: Rain-related with no bathroom above it does point to roof or attic. We'd want to do a roof inspection and check the attic for moisture.
Caller: Can you do both in one visit?
Agent: Absolutely. One visit, one assessment. We'll give you a clear picture of what's happening and what it'll cost to fix.
Caller: Great, I need this figured out before it gets worse.
expectedPoints: rain-related likely roof or attic source
